scope: B tasks below 20, processed one task at a time

accepted.status: accepted but still 12 cost units above the 20-point boundary

attempts: Move Slice and Pad constants to Constant nodes: valid but cost rises to 333 because constants count as both parameters and activations.
Two-nonzero sparse offset Conv: theoretical cost 100, rejected by official ONNX full checker because Conv does not accept sparse_tensor(float) as W.
Legacy Slice attributes: Slice-9 works, but Pad-9 does not support bool and ai.onnx cannot be imported as a separate versioned standard domain.

rejected_or_blocked.finding: The final zero-activation Einsum uses minimal row rank 3 and column rank 4. Explicit 9x9 construction already exceeds the 148 budget.
A grouped Conv reduces identity weights but cannot route input channel 8 to output channels 0 and 2. Sparse Conv is rejected by full checker.

The previous quantized outer product has order (ab,xy), not required Kronecker order (ax,by). Correcting it with explicit transpose/reshape tensors costs more than the baseline; a one-channel dynamic ConvTranspose cannot express the background OR condition.
rejected_or_blocked.status: no accepted replacement in this round

rejected_or_blocked.finding: The model has zero activation cost and factorizes a fixed four-way spatial fold plus a channel conflict suppressor. Dropping the channel factor is known to fail all 266 examples; explicit fold operators create full-grid activations.
rejected_or_blocked.status: no accepted replacement in this round
